reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures the informal question. A "straight line" is encoded as y = mx + b, which only excludes vertical lines; vertical lines can intersect the graph of a polynomial function in at most one point, so this restriction cannot change the answer. The set {x : mx + b = polynomial(x)} counts x-coordinates of intersection points, and since each intersection point's x-coordinate uniquely determines it, this matches counting distinct intersection points. Using Set.encard = 4 (verified from Mathlib's Data/Set/Card.lean as cardinality in ℕ∞) expresses "exactly 4 intersection points", which for a degree-4 polynomial (which has at most 4 real roots) coincides with "at least 4 distinct intersection points" — the natural reading of the informal claim. The formalization is therefore strictly faithful.
